message:
chore(beads): sync issue tracker state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -41,11 +41,13 @@
 {"id":"br-2ei","title":"MCP Agent Mail Rust: 100% feature parity + conformance + benchmarks","description":"Goal: complete the Rust port of legacy_python_mcp_agent_mail_code with 100% behavior/feature coverage, proven by fixture-based conformance tests + benchmarks (same bar as rich_rust/beads_rust).

Non-negotiables:
- Prefer local crates over upstream ecosystem where applicable:
  - MCP: /dp/fastmcp_rust
  - SQLite: /dp/sqlmodel_rust
  - IO/concurrency: /dp/asupersync (avoid tokio unless unavoidable)
  - Console UI: /dp/frankentui
  - Beads: /dp/beads_rust
  - Agent detection: /dp/coding_agent_session_search
- No destructive commands or file deletions without explicit permission.

Acceptance criteria:
- cargo fmt --check passes
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes
- cargo test passes
- Conformance harness covers all MCP tools + resources (including error cases and nondeterministic-field normalization)
- Benchmarks cover DB/tool hot paths and, once implemented, archive write throughput
","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:05:51.223489285Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T05:05:51.223489285Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-2ei.1","title":"Conformance Harness: cover all tools/resources + artifact parity","description":"Expand conformance tests so they prove real feature parity, not just API-shape parity.

Scope:
- Ensure python_reference.json includes cases for every MCP tool registered by Rust server (currently missing: force_release_file_reservation, install_precommit_guard, uninstall_precommit_guard).
- Add negative/error cases per tool where legacy behavior is defined (invalid params, CONTACT_BLOCKED flows, etc).
- Add resource query-param variants where legacy supports them.
- Add artifact-level assertions once storage/archive layer exists (message markdown files, inbox/outbox copies, file_reservation JSON artifacts, attachment manifests).

Acceptance:
- `cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-conformance` fails on any behavioral drift.
- Fixtures are deterministic: normalize/ignore only genuinely nondeterministic fields (timestamps, hashes), and document why.

Key files:
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-conformance/tests/conformance/python_reference/generate_fixtures.py
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-conformance/tests/conformance/fixtures/python_reference.json
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-conformance/tests/conformance.rs
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:06:15.213350684Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T05:06:15.213350684Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.1","depends_on_id":"br-2ei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2ei.1.1","title":"Fixtures: missing tool coverage (guard install/uninstall + force_release)","description":"Track conformance fixture coverage for the last missing MCP tools (as reported by python_reference.json). This issue is a meta-epic; individual tools get their own child tasks with concrete fixture scenarios, normalization rules, and Rust runner integration.

Missing tools to cover:
- install_precommit_guard
- uninstall_precommit_guard
- force_release_file_reservation

Non-negotiables:
- Add BOTH positive and negative/error-path cases.
- Record expected shapes from legacy Python and keep fixtures deterministic (normalize only true nondeterminism like timestamps/paths).
- Include detailed logs in the fixture generator so failures are diagnosable.

Child tasks under this epic should each include:
- Legacy behavior matrix (inputs -> expected outputs)
- Fixture generator changes
- Rust conformance runner changes
- Any required normalization rules documented inline","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:10:18.916319777Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T05:51:37.895534917Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.1.1","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"br-2ei.1.1.1","title":"Fixtures: precommit guard install/uninstall tool cases","description":"Add conformance fixture coverage for:
- install_precommit_guard(project_key, code_repo_path)
- uninstall_precommit_guard(code_repo_path)

Fixture scenarios (must be stable + diagnostic):
- Install into a fresh git repo (no existing hooks): succeeds; chain-runner + plugin present.
- Install idempotency: second install is a no-op (or same result) and does not clobber preserved hook state.
- Install when repo already has a pre-commit hook: legacy-preserve behavior matches (e.g., .orig + chain-runner semantics).
- Uninstall: removes only agent-mail components and restores preserved hook when applicable.
- Uninstall idempotency: running twice is safe.
- Error paths: non-existent path, not-a-git-repo dir, permission denied / read-only hooks dir.

Normalization rules:
- Paths must be stored as relative or redacted (never machine-absolute).
- Timestamps should be normalized if present.

Implementation:
- Extend python_reference fixture generator to create a temp repo and exercise the tools.
- Update Rust conformance runner to execute these cases and compare to fixtures.
- Log each subcase with: repo layout summary, expected vs actual tool result, and any created hook paths.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:52:01.241854819Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T05:52:01.241854819Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.1.1.1","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.1.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:52:01.241854819Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.1.1.1","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:52:01.241854819Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"br-2ei.1.1.2","title":"Fixtures: force_release_file_reservation tool cases","description":"Add conformance fixture coverage for:
- force_release_file_reservation(project_key, agent_name, file_reservation_id, notify_previous, note)

Fixture scenarios (must be deterministic):
- DENY: reservation is not abandoned (recent agent activity / recent mail / recent archive activity).
- ALLOW: reservation appears abandoned per legacy heuristics; reservation is released and archive artifacts updated.
- ALLOW + notify_previous=true: a notification message is sent to the previous holder including the provided note + heuristic summary.
- Edge cases:
  - reservation already released -> no-op vs error (match legacy)
  - reservation expired -> expected behavior (match legacy)
  - invalid reservation id -> error shape parity

Determinism strategy:
- In the fixture generator, explicitly set timestamps/last_active markers to known fixed values.
- Normalize nondeterministic fields (timestamps, commit SHAs) only if the legacy fixture includes them.

Implementation:
- Extend python_reference fixture generator to set up agents + reservations + activity signals, then call the tool.
- Update Rust conformance runner to execute these cases and compare.
- Log each subcase with the measured heuristic signals and final allow/deny.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:52:13.014200455Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T05:52:13.014200455Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.1.1.2","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.1.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:52:13.014200455Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.1.1.2","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.2.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:52:13.014200455Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2ei.1.2","title":"Fixtures: add negative/error cases (invalid params, contact enforcement, policy coercions)","description":"Add high-signal error cases to conformance so we lock in edge behavior.

Candidates:
- ensure_project: relative path -> error
- register_agent: invalid name under strict enforcement -> error or coercion depending on config
- send_message: CONTACT_BLOCKED / CONTACT_REQUIRED flows
- file_reservation_paths: conflicts returned shape; TTL min enforcement
- renew_file_reservations: invalid ids/paths
- search_messages: FTS syntax vs LIKE fallback edge cases
- resources: missing required query params -> error

Approach:
- Add cases to python fixture generator where legacy behavior is clear.
- Normalize only nondeterministic fields.

Acceptance:
- Tests fail on any drift.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:10:53.695321528Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T05:38:33.464730817Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T05:38:33.464713414Z","close_reason":"Added negative/error conformance cases and updated fixtures/generator","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.1.2","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2ei.1.3","title":"Conformance: assert git archive artifacts (messages/inbox/outbox/reservations/attachments) match legacy","description":"Extend conformance beyond JSON tool outputs to verify archive side effects.

Artifacts to assert after running a fixture script:
- messages/YYYY/MM/*.md canonical exists; parses frontmatter JSON; body matches expected.
- agents/<Agent>/inbox + outbox copies exist and match canonical.
- file_reservations/*.json artifacts exist for created reservations.
- attachments manifests + WebP/originals as expected.

Strategy:
- Fixture generator should record expected relative paths and key frontmatter fields, not absolute machine paths.
- Use hashing for large binaries; compare sha256.
- Allow nondeterministic timestamps but assert ordering and presence.

Dependencies:
- Requires storage layer implementation.
","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:11:01.868813386Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T05:11:08.695099421Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.1.3","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.1.3","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.1.3","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.1.3","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2ei.2","title":"Storage: Git archive write/read pipeline + attachments","description":"Implement the git-backed mailbox archive described in EXISTING_MCP_AGENT_MAIL_STRUCTURE.md.

This is the core missing subsystem: legacy Python writes messages + file reservations + attachments into a per-project git repo and uses it as an auditable artifact store.

Scope (must match legacy behavior):
- Archive root creation + .gitattributes
- Per-project advisory locks (.archive.lock + owner metadata)
- Commit batching queue (max 10, max wait 50ms)
- Message write pipeline:
  - canonical messages/YYYY/MM/*.md
  - agents/<Agent>/inbox + outbox copies
  - thread digest files (messages/threads/<thread_id>.md) if present in legacy
  - git commit message format
- File reservation artifacts (file_reservations/<sha1(pattern)>.json + id-<id>.json)
- Attachment pipeline:
  - compute content hashes
  - convert images to WebP when enabled
  - inline small attachments (< 64KiB) into markdown
  - keep originals when configured
  - write manifests + audit logs
- Read helpers for resources that include commit metadata.

Constraints:
- Prefer git2 library (already in workspace deps); avoid shelling out when possible.
- Prefer asupersync for IO/concurrency.

Acceptance:
- New conformance layer can assert archive artifacts exactly match legacy (file layout + frontmatter JSON + markdown body).
- Benchmarks can measure write throughput and commit batching.
","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:06:24.771240880Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T05:06:24.771240880Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.2","depends_on_id":"br-2ei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2ei.2.1","title":"Archive: initialize archive root + per-project git repos + .gitattributes","description":"Implement archive initialization in mcp-agent-mail-storage.

Requirements:
- Determine archive root from Config.storage_root (default ~/.mcp_agent_mail_git_mailbox_repo).
- Ensure root exists.
- For each project slug, ensure a git repo exists at <root>/<slug>/.
- Ensure expected top-level dirs exist: agents/, messages/, attachments/, file_reservations/.
- Write .gitattributes (at least: enforce LF for *.md and binary for images).
- Ensure git author identity set from config (name/email) for commits.

Implementation:
- Use git2 to init and commit initial scaffolding if repo newly created.

Acceptance:
- Idempotent: calling multiple times does not change repo.
- Unit test with temp dir.
","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:09:03.245769103Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T05:51:33.438606780Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T05:51:33.438544202Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.2.1","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-2ei.2.2","title":"Archive: per-project advisory locks + commit batching queue","description":"Implement concurrency control for archive writes.

Spec:
- Per-project advisory lock file: <project>/.archive.lock (+ optional owner metadata JSON).
- Commit queue batching: max 10 commits or max wait 50ms.
- Stale lock cleanup on startup.

Implementation notes:
- Use filesystem lock (cross-platform) + busy-timeout retry. Prefer asupersync primitives if available.
- Keep API simple for callers: `with_project_lock(project_slug, || ...)`.
- Batching should preserve ordering for operations within a single process.

Acceptance:
- Unit tests simulate concurrent lock acquisition.
- Bench: lock acquisition overhead is low.

 {"id":"br-2ei.2.3","title":"Archive: message write pipeline (canonical + inbox/outbox + commit) + frontmatter format","description":"Implement message archival writes that mirror legacy Python.

Spec (EXISTING_MCP_AGENT_MAIL_STRUCTURE.md):
- Canonical: messages/YYYY/MM/<timestamp>__<subject_slug>__<id>.md
- Outbox: agents/<sender>/outbox/YYYY/MM/... (same rel path)
- Inboxes: agents/<recipient>/inbox/YYYY/MM/...
- Frontmatter: JSON preceded by ---json header line, then Markdown body.
- Git commit summary: "message <id> from <sender> to <recipients>".

Inputs:
- Message record + recipients + markdown body + attachments metadata.

Outputs:
- Files written atomically.
- Git commit performed (or batched) and commit info stored/returned where needed.

Acceptance:
- Deterministic path + slug generation.
- Conformance can assert files exist and parse.
","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:09:23.321591344Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T05:09:28.412372534Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.2.3","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.2.3","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.2.3","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2ei.2.4","title":"Archive: file reservation artifacts (sha1(pattern).json + id-<id>.json)","description":"Implement archive writes for file reservations.

Spec:
- file_reservations/<sha1(path_pattern)>.json
- file_reservations/id-<id>.json

Requirements:
- JSON includes {id, agent, path_pattern, exclusive, reason, created_ts, expires_ts, released_ts} at minimum.
- Writes are atomic.
- Archive writes happen on reservation create/update/release and are committed (or batched).

Acceptance:
- Resource `resource://file_reservations/{slug}` can be backed by archive artifacts (optional) and/or DB.
- Conformance can assert artifact existence.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:09:35.083785229Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T05:09:43.133923445Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.2.4","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.2.4","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2ei.2.4","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-2ei.2.5","title":"Archive: attachment pipeline (hashing, WebP conversion, manifests, inline small)","description":"Implement attachment handling for both tool message sending and share/export.

Spec:
- attachments/<sha1[:2]>/<sha1>.webp
- attachments/originals/<sha1[:2]>/<sha1>.<ext>
- attachments/_manifests/<sha1>.json
- attachments/_audit/<sha1>.log

Behavior:
- Convert images to WebP when enabled; disable when stderr not tty? (mirror legacy policy).
- Inline attachments <= 64KiB into markdown; store larger attachments as files and reference via manifest.
- Optionally keep originals.
- Ensure deterministic hashing + stable paths.

Acceptance:
- Conformance can assert archive artifacts and message frontmatter attachment refs.
- Works cross-platform.

 {"id":"br-2ei.7.2","title":"Bench: archive write throughput (message + attachments + commit batching)","description":"Add benchmarks for archive writes.

Dependencies:
- Requires storage layer write pipeline + attachments + batching (br-2ei.2.*).

Work:

- Measure end-to-end: write canonical+inbox/outbox + commit batching.
- Report throughput (msgs/sec) and p95 latency.

 {"id":"br-2ei.8","title":"Docs: add Rust-port README + dev workflow (fmt/clippy/test/conformance/bench)","description":"Add a top-level README.md for this Rust port.

Must include:
- What the project is (Rust port of mcp_agent_mail)
- How to run MCP server: stdio and HTTP
- How to run conformance tests
- How to regenerate fixtures (legacy venv python command)
- How to run benches
- Notes on per-agent CARGO_TARGET_DIR to avoid multi-agent target lock contention

Acceptance:
- README is accurate and matches current code + scripts.
","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:07:09.183372429Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T05:38:39.476350794Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T05:38:39.476334223Z","close_reason":"Added top-level README with run/test/conformance/bench instructions","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.8","depends_on_id":"br-2ei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:37:36Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"br-2ei.9","title":"E2E Test Suite: harness + scripts + artifacts","description":"Add a first-class end-to-end (E2E) harness for MCP Agent Mail Rust, focused on *user-visible correctness* and diagnosability.

Goals:
- Exercise the system the way real agents/users do: CLI -> server -> DB -> git archive -> share export -> guard hooks.
- Produce extremely detailed logs and artifacts so failures are obvious (expected vs actual, file diffs/hashes, HTTP transcripts).
- Keep runs deterministic and CI-friendly.

Standards:
- Scripts live under scripts/ (bash, cross-platform aware where feasible).
- Artifacts live under tests/artifacts/<suite>/<timestamp>/.
- Every script must:
  - Print a clear case header per check
  - Log expected vs actual
  - Exit non-zero on first mismatch
  - Support AM_E2E_KEEP_TMP=1 to retain temp dirs
  - Respect per-agent CARGO_TARGET_DIR to avoid multi-agent contention
- No destructive git/fs commands; only operate on temp dirs.

This epic owns the harness + the core E2E scripts for archive, guard, share/export, and CLI stability.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:52:30.044860332Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T05:52:30.044860332Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.9","depends_on_id":"br-2ei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:52:30.044860332Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"br-2ei.9.1","title":"E2E: harness scaffolding (runner + log helpers + artifacts)","description":"Create the shared E2E harness used by all other E2E scripts.

Deliverables:
- scripts/e2e_test.sh: top-level runner (can run all suites or a named suite).
- scripts/e2e_lib.sh: helpers for:
  - mktemp workspace + cleanup (AM_E2E_KEEP_TMP=1)
  - artifact dir selection (tests/artifacts/<suite>/<timestamp>/)
  - logging (case banners, expected vs actual)
  - stable hashing + file tree dumps
  - retry helpers for flaky ports/binds

Requirements:
- Detailed logging by default; include env dump (redacting secrets).
- All scripts must be safe (no destructive git/fs commands).
- The runner must set CARGO_TARGET_DIR if not already set (recommended per-agent directory).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:52:47.452322784Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T05:52:47.452322784Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2ei.9.1","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T05:52:47.452322784Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

  - Agent detection: /dp/coding_agent_session_search
- No destructive commands or file deletions without explicit permission.
